LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SYSTEM ENERGY RESOURCES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COURT HAS JURISDICTION TO CONSIDER AND RESOLVE PLAINTIFF'S CHALLENGES TO THE ARBITRATION APPRAISAL AWARD. IN ADDITION TO THE COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF HAS FILED AND SERVED A MOTION TO VACATE THAT WAS TIMELY FILED. THE COMPLAINT AND MOTION MEET THE MATERIAL SUBSTANTIVE AND PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS AND CONDITIONS OF A PETITION TO VACATE UNDER THE CALIFORNIA ARBITRATION ACT, EXCEPT FOR ITS TITLE. THE HEARING ON THE MOTION TO VACATE APPRAISAL AWARD SET ON JANUARY 17 IS CONTINUED BY THE COURT TO JANUARY 28, 2014 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SYSTEM ENERGY RESOURCES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. The Court has jurisdiction to consider and resolve Plaintiff's challenges to the arbitration appraisal award. In addition to the Complaint, Plaintiff has filed and served a motion to vacate that was timely filed. The Complaint and motion meet the material substantive and procedural requirements and conditions of a petition to vacate under the California Arbitration Act, except for its title. The hearing on the Motion to vacate Appraisal Award set on January 17 is continued by the court to January 28, 2014 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
